mission.title: Install and Configure MetalLB (Bare-metal Load Balancer)
mission.description: MetalLB is a load-balancer implementation for bare metal Kubernetes clusters, utilizing standard routing protocols. It is particularly useful in environments where cloud load balancers are not available, providing a way to expose services externally. Key trade-offs include the need for manual configuration of IP address pools and potential complexity in managing network routing.

steps.title: Add the MetalLB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official MetalLB Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add metallb https://metallb.universe.tf

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the MetalLB repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install MetalLB CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by MetalLB to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metallb/metallb/v0.15.3/manifests/namespace.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metallb/metallb/v0.15.3/manifests/metallb.yaml

```

> Creates the MetalLB namespace.
> Deploys MetalLB components.

steps.title: Install MetalLB using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs MetalLB into the Kubernetes cluster using Helm, specifying the namespace.

```bash

helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

```

> Installs MetalLB in the metallb-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a MetalLB ConfigMap
steps.description:
This step creates a ConfigMap to define the IP address pool that MetalLB will use for load balancing.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    layer2:
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

```

> Replace the IP range with your own.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    layer2:
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

steps.title: Verify MetalLB installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the MetalLB pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

```

> You should see MetalLB controller and speaker pods running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MetalLB
uninstall.description:
This step removes MetalLB from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss if services are relying on MetalLB.

```bash

helm uninstall metallb --namespace metallb-system

kubectl delete namespace metallb-system

```

> Uninstalls MetalLB Helm release.
> Removes the MetalLB namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MetalLB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configuration. This step upgrades MetalLB to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

helm upgrade metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

```

> Backup current MetalLB configuration.
> Upgrades MetalLB to the specified version.
> Verify that the MetalLB pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'service is not exposed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service is not configured correctly to use MetalLB.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service type is set to LoadBalancer and the MetalLB ConfigMap is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: MetalLB pods are in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be a misconfiguration in the MetalLB ConfigMap.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the MetalLB controller pod for errors using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n metallb-system'.

troubleshooting.title: No IP addresses assigned to services
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The IP address range defined in the ConfigMap may not be valid or available.

**Fix:**
Verify that the IP range in the ConfigMap is correct and not in use by other devices.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to allocate IP address'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** All IP addresses in the defined range are already in use.

**Fix:**
Expand the IP address range in the MetalLB ConfigMap or free up some IP addresses.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for BGP peering with multiple routers, improved logging for troubleshooting.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for upgrading from v0.15.2.

resolution.summary: You should see the MetalLB controller and speaker pods running with 'kubectl get pods -n metallb-system' showing 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
